rulings.Case Title: ELLEN PAO VS. KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Oppos To Pltf'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Ptf'S First Request Fro Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 23, 2014, Line 1 - DEFENDANT KLEINER PERKINS CAUFIELD & BYERS LLC's Motion To Seal Documents Filed In Support Of Oppos To Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses To Plaintiff's First Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is granted without opposition. The Court finds facts establishing an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record and a substantial probability that interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALDJIA HALTIM-BERCHE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn, Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 23, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Denied. The grounds for a motion for judgment on the pleadings "shall appear on the face of the challenged pleading or from any matter of which the court is required to take judicial notice." (CCP sec. 438.) Where the Court is asked to take judicial notice, the particular matters to be noticed must be specified either in the notice of motion or supporting points and authorities. (Id., see also Weil & Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civil Proc. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2013) par. 7:315.) Defendant does not request that the Court take judicial notice of any facts. The alleged statute of limitations defect does not appear on the face of the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: KATHY YOUNT, INDIVIDUALLY AND IN HER CAPACITY AS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication On All Claims
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 23, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication On All Claims. The motion is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish the wrongful death cause of action. Plaintiff fails to highlight any facts creating a duty of care under the traditional Rowland v. Christian, (1968) 69 Cal.2d 108, 113, factors or based on unusual police conduct that created a special relationship between Mr. Yount and defendant. See Adams v. City of Fremont (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 243. Defendant did not encourage Mr. Yount to jump. Defendant did not owe a duty to arrest the hecklers; defendant was focused on Mr. Yount and securing the scene. While Officer Perez called Mr. Yount a fool, he told him to go back inside. Plaintiff speculates that Mr. Yount heard defendant and the hecklers that encouraged Mr. Yount to jump. Lugtu v. California Highway Patrol (2001) 26 Cal.4th 703, is distinguishable. In that action, the police officer increased the risk of danger to the injured party. Plaintiff cannot establish the Bane Act cause of action and cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. Those claims were not part of plaintiff's government claim. See Nelson v. County of Los Angeles (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 783, 796. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: KEN LAM et al VS. CALIFORNIA CAPITAL INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 23, 2014, Line 9: DEFENDANTS WANG INSURANCE AGENCY, INC. and SIMON GIANG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled as to the cause of action for breach of contract as Plaintiff sufficiently pled the legal effect of the contract. (Construction Protective Services, Inc. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 199; Khoury v. Maly's of Cal. (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) Demurrer is also overruled as to the fraud and negligent misrepresentation causes of action because the Complaint sufficiently alleges each required element for both causes of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. EDWIN JUCUTAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 23, 2014, Line 11: PLAINTIFF PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. Continued to July 31, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a over letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 23, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/AJR)
